issues.title: docs: v0.11.0 alpha ship postmortem + running lessons-learned
issues.body:
## What

Adds `docs/POSTMORTEM_v0.11.0_ALPHA.md` -- **entry #1 of a running lessons-learned log**
for P(Doom)1 ship postmortems (future ships append dated entries; prior entries are never
rewritten).

Frames the log against the self-documenting-project vision
(`VISION_ACCUMULATION_AND_EVOLUTION.md` 2.4): the repo recording its own developmental
lessons is the accumulation thesis in **process-space**, the same shape of story as the
player-facing strategy accumulation.

## Entry #1 -- v0.11.0 friends-and-family Windows alpha (2026-07-17..20)

1. **What we set out to ship** -- the LOOP + META-LOOP (download -> play -> lose -> score
   posts -> lands on a live global leaderboard friends can see); live PHP score API on the
   DreamCompute VM, frozen contract (ADR-0002).
2. **The leaderboard-crash hunt (headline) -- five passes to root cause**, told honestly:
   endgame freeze + lost score (#705) -> memory leak (#713) -> load perf (#716) -> renderer
   ruled out (`--rendering-driver opengl3`) -> texture-decode segfault (#728). Each pass a
   real bug; only the last was the crash. Includes the honest caveat that #728's static
   analysis did **not** confirm a malformed PNG (Godot codec edge-case *or* misleading
   last-log-line) -- flagged so no later ship cites it as settled.
3. **Lessons (reusable core)** -- headless GUT cannot catch GUI render/texture-decode
   crashes (human release-build playtest was the only gate that surfaced it); native-segfault
   triage toolkit (`--verbose` last-resource, `opengl3` renderer-vs-content, debug masks
   release-only crashes); do not declare "fixed" without the real repro passing; process
   landmines (background-commit rollback, `git checkout -- godot/`, machine-sleep stalls).
4. **What went right** -- live backend round-trip-verified; real fixes shipped; anti-hollow +
   property tests (ADR-0017); vision doc captured; working art pipeline. The human playtest
   catching what tests could not is a **feature of the process, not a failure**.
5. **Actions carried forward** -- #715, #717/#707/#602/#565, #714, #712, #703, #700, #718,
   plus the #728 batch-risk re-test.

Ship status noted as of 2026-07-20: core loop verified; final texture-crash fix merged
(#728) but not yet re-tested on a fresh release build; **publish pending Pip's re-test.**

Docs-only, ASCII-clean, one file. **Do not merge -- for Pip's review.**

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Docs: correct PLAYERGUIDE tutorial claims to match the Godot build (SSOT declaration)
issues.body:
Context: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md ("New Player Tutorial and Help System") claims a comprehensive tutorial system, Factorio-style hints, an interactive walkthrough, per-mechanic first-time popups, and a press-H help key. A code search of godot/ finds none of this (no tutorial/onboard files, no TutorialManager, no hints_enabled flag). Stale pygame-era aspirational copy.

This issue: make the doc truthful and promote it to the single source of truth for player documentation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Remove or mark as "planned" every claim not implemented in the Godot build.
- Add a note that pdoom1 is the SSOT and content flows outward (consistent with #545 and docs/sync-config.yml).
- Keep the accurate content (resources, actions, AP strategy, events, travel, game over, seeds).
- No code changes.

Pointers: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md, README.md, issue #545. See docs/strategy/PLAYER_DOCS_PIPELINE_2026-07-20.md (PR #718). Size: S (Phase 0).

issues.title: Cross-repo sync: fold player-guide mapping into the #545 cleanup pass
issues.body:
Context: #545 rewrites docs/sync-config.yml and sync-documentation.yml, removing dead no-op mappings. Today only docs/shared/* flows, and only into siblings' docs/ (dev-visible, not public/).

This issue: while #545 edits those files, add one real live mapping for the player guide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Mapping: SSOT player guide -> website public/ (player-visible), per the website-publish issue.
- Verify with a trivial change that the website receives it (no reintroduced dead mapping).
- Token choice (CROSS_REPO_TOKEN vs WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN) consistent with #545's consolidation decision.

Pointers: docs/sync-config.yml, .github/workflows/sync-documentation.yml, .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml, issue #545. Size: S.

issues.title: Website: publish the player guide to pdoom1.com with pdoom1 -> website sync
issues.body:
Context: pdoom1-website is static (netlify publishes public/). public/docs/ is developer-facing; there is no player how-to-play page and nothing renders the game's PLAYERGUIDE. A proven push-to-public pattern exists: .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml pushes into website public/blog/ via WEBSITE_SYNC_TOKEN.

Acceptance criteria:
- Player-facing How to Play page under website public/ (e.g. public/docs/how-to-play), sourced from the game repo PLAYERGUIDE, with a nav link.
- Game-repo workflow modeled on sync-dev-blog.yml (NOT the leaderboard PHP path) pushing rendered output on change.
- No hand-maintained copy in the website repo.

Pointers: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md, .github/workflows/sync-dev-blog.yml, website netlify.toml, website public/docs/. Coordinate with #545. Size: M (Phase 4).

issues.title: Onboarding: opt-in Tutorial Mode (relaunchable guided steps)
issues.body:
Context: no tutorial engine exists in godot/ (verified). Builds on the first-launch skeleton and hint data.

Acceptance criteria:
- TutorialManager autoload driving an ordered step overlay (highlight target, instruction, advance on action or Next).
- Offered on first launch; relaunchable via a Tutorial entry on the welcome menu.
- tutorial_completed persisted in GameConfig; never forced on returning players.
- Cover the six PLAYERGUIDE areas (resources, actions, attention, turn management, events/milestones, upgrades).

Pointers: godot/autoload/, godot/scripts/ui/welcome_screen.gd, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd, docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md. Depends on the skeleton + hints issues. Size: L (Phase 3).

issues.title: Onboarding: data-driven contextual hints (Factorio-style show-once triggers)
issues.body:
Context: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md already names four first-time triggers (first hire, first upgrade, out of Action Points/Attention, high doom). None implemented. Game is data-driven (godot/data/*.json); hints should be data, not hardcoded.

Acceptance criteria:
- godot/data/help/hints.json: entries {id, title, body, trigger, guide_anchor}.
- HintManager autoload firing each hint once, gated by GameConfig.show_hints, shown ids persisted.
- Wire the four PLAYERGUIDE triggers.
- Unit test: every guide_anchor resolves to a real PLAYERGUIDE section (fast gate).

Pointers: docs/PLAYERGUIDE.md, godot/data/, godot/autoload/, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd. Depends on the first-launch skeleton issue. Size: M (Phase 2, highest gameplay ROI).

issues.title: Onboarding: first-launch help layer skeleton (settings flag + dismissible overlay)
issues.body:
Context: the only in-play onboarding is getting_started_hint in godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd (visible turn < 3). godot/autoload/game_config.gd already has games_played (free first-launch detector) and last_seen_version (proven show-once gate, see whats_new_modal.gd).

Acceptance criteria:
- Add show_hints: bool = true to GameConfig, persisted, exposed as a "Show gameplay hints" settings toggle.
- On first launch (games_played == 0) show one dismissible welcome/help overlay pointing at the Player Guide.
- Respect the toggle everywhere hints show.
- Reuse the last_seen_version show-once pattern.

Pointers: godot/autoload/game_config.gd, godot/scripts/ui/main_ui.gd, godot/scripts/ui/whats_new_modal.gd, godot/scripts/ui/welcome_screen.gd. See PLAYER_DOCS_PIPELINE_2026-07-20.md (PR #718). Size: S (Phase 1).

issues.title: fix(text): player-facing copy audit -- traps fixed + hardcoded->variable
issues.body:
Player-facing text audit. Full findings + rationale in `docs/game-design/TEXT_AUDIT_2026-07-20.md`. All flagged items were triaged with Pip and his rulings applied.

## Traps fixed
- **T1** Papers tooltip dropped the false "-3 doom each" (`actions.gd:341` prices paper doom-reduction at 0 under ADR-0015). Now: "Publications boost reputation (+N each) and may have further downstream impact as their findings are adopted."
- **T2** Removed the "Hire SR" dev/test button (`TestActionButton`) that was live in the control bar and hired a researcher directly.
- **T3** Removed the vestigial "Init" button (the game auto-boots from `_ready`; handler renamed `_on_init_button_pressed` -> `_boot_game`). Confirmed via git history it was the Phase-4-MVP start button, now superseded.
- **T4** ">> INITIALIZE LAB" -> "Launch Lab" (`pregame_setup.tscn`, `config_confirmation.tscn`), matching the welcome screen.
- **T5** What's-New fallback no longer points at `CHANGELOG.md` -> "Visit pdoom1.com". Filed **pdoom1-website#141** to create a real player-facing changelog page (the website has none today).

## Hardcoded -> variable
- **H1** `config_confirmation.gd` starting-funding display now reads `Balance.num("starting_resources.money")` instead of a hardcoded `245000`.
- **H2** Economy HUD tooltips (compute / research / papers / AP) are now built from `Balance` at runtime, and `turn_manager` reads the same new keys (`papers.research_per_paper`, `papers.reputation_per_paper`, `compute.per_researcher_per_turn`; `action_points.per_staff` already existed) -- **single source, no drift**. Balance defaults equal the old literals, so behaviour is unchanged.

## Verification
- Fast gate: **510 tests, 0 failures**.
- Simulation/determinism tier (turn_manager is economy code): **96 tests, 0 failures**.

Do NOT merge -- copy/design decisions are Pip's. Cross-repo follow-up: pdoom1-website#141.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: art(ui): directional menu-consistency pass (palette/texture alignment)
issues.body:
## What

A COHERENCE pass over every menu/screen -- not a redesign. Aligns menu chrome
to the established direction (warm-grime base + heavy-outline heft, dark
indigo/aubergine grounds + cozy-amber accent) per `docs/art/palette.json`,
`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`, and the overnight sweep rulings.

Full menu-by-menu audit + ranked findings + needs-Pips-eye list:
`docs/game-design/UI_MENU_CONSISTENCY_2026-07-20.md`.

## Applied (low-risk, directional: colors + one texture swap only)

- Retired the copy-pasted **slate-blue button stylebox** set across welcome,
  settings, pregame, config-confirmation, player-guide -> doom-indigo fill +
  heavy aubergine outline, amber on hover/press/focus.
- **Ice-blue section headers/titles** -> cozy amber / off-white.
- **Modal panels** (pause, whats-new, bug-report, player-guide) -> deep
  aubergine grounds + dimmed amber frames.
- **Welcome overlay**: green scanlines -> amber scanlines (existing asset;
  green is reserved for the "computers OK" semantic); AI-Safety link button
  -> teal UI-action token.
- **In-game HUD** stragglers unified to the TerminalTheme amber register;
  plan-screen resting purple retired (purple is doctrine-reserved for
  band 3+ eldritch).
- Recorded the new menu chrome tokens in `godot/UI_STYLE_GUIDE.md`.

No layout, node, or @onready-path changes anywhere; every screen stays
functional.

## Flagged for Pip (NOT changed)

Leaderboard + keybind screens are unthemed (default Godot controls); the real
fix is a shared `menu_theme.tres` rather than more copy-paste. Also: emoji in
labels, leaderboard verdigris background, and several script-side stragglers
(theme_manager `apply_button_style` still emits electric-blue/neon-magenta).
See the report's "Needs Pip's eye" section.

## Verify

Fast gate: **510 tests, 0 failures, 57/57 files** (`--quick --ci-mode
--min-tests 300`). A stray first run showed 5 failures from stale `user://`
leaderboard JSON in this worktree; confirmed by stashing the edits (same 5
failed with a clean tree) and they cleared on re-run -- unrelated to the retone.

Do NOT merge -- Pip eyeballs UI.

issues.title: Perf: turn-1 / early-days processing feels slow (watch-and-track)
issues.body:
From Pip's v0.11.0 re-test (2026-07-20): "it also seemed to take a while on the first few days inside the turn cycle on the first turn -- maybe something to keep an eye on."

Early-game (turn 1 / first few day-ticks) has a noticeable processing pause. Not blocking, but worth a profiling pass: likely one-time setup work (event deck load, rival-lab init, baseline schedule, RNG warm-up) happening synchronously on the first turn. Options once profiled: defer/async the heavy init, spread it across the pre-game load, or surface it as a brief loading moment (ties into the loading-screen/stats-display idea + the accumulation vision doc #712).

Track alongside the leaderboard open-perf work (fix/leaderboard-open-perf-and-clear-label). Log timings when convenient.

issues.title: docs(vision): accumulation + evolution -- run telemetry as the game's self-building substrate
issues.body:
## What

Adds `docs/game-design/VISION_ACCUMULATION_AND_EVOLUTION.md` -- a living vision doc capturing Pip's north-star framing for what P(Doom)1's data *becomes* over time, then bolts architectural structure onto it.

Pip's words are preserved verbatim (core attraction, "things accumulate ... like runs on pdoom1", the aggregate-and-visualise ask, the self-building motif, the tree-of-life / Cambrian-explosion image). The coordinator's synthesis is layered on top:

1. **Run telemetry as the substrate** -- the leaderboard is the first instance of a run-telemetry system, not a scoreboard. `VerificationTracker`'s chained SHA-256 hash is the run's DNA; the `pdoom1-replay-v1` artifact (ADR-0006) is its shape.
2. **Contract-safe enrichment path** -- optionally carry the run's shape (input log + strategy signature + hash) as a *sibling telemetry channel*. The frozen score contract (ADR-0002) stays backward-compatible; enrichment is additive and ignorable.
3. **The visualisations it unlocks** (named as future work, not built): strategy-fingerprint histograms, a forking-channels strategy tree, deep-run replays, population-level exploit-discovery detection.
4. **The self-referential motif** -- the accumulating run-data and the accumulating ADR/change-history are the same shape of story; cross-links `DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY.md` and the `pdoom-data` A/B/C/D tiers as the sibling data plane.
5. **Near-term hooks** -- small do-now capture moves so the substrate accretes before any viz exists; keep ALL of it (explicit no-cleanup constraint).

Clearly separates **the dream** (aspirational, unbuilt) from **buildable next hooks**. Does NOT propose changing the frozen score contract.

## Note

This is Pip's own design philosophy -- **left for Pip to review and merge himself**, not for agent admin-merge.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: UI consistency sweep after asset promotion + office-sim integration (level-2 visual pass)
issues.body:
Pip's forward-looking concern (2026-07-19), captured so it isn't lost.

## The two levels of the art pipeline
- **Level 1 (building now):** the review app's Keep/Reroll wiring PROMOTES asset files into `godot/assets/` and feeds Rerolls into the next generation wave. Makes assets *available*.
- **Level 2 (this issue):** the game UI must actually *reference and render* the promoted assets, the office simulator gets folded onto a real game screen, and then -- crucially -- a CONSISTENCY SWEEP over ALL UI elements.

## The sweep
Once the new assets are promoted + the office-sim is integrated + round-2 UI improvements land, audit every UI element/screen for visual coherence against the locked style: warm-grime + heavy-outline heft + deeper shadow; palette from `docs/art/palette.json`; consistent icon stroke/sizing; panel-frame treatment; spacing; the doom-as-additive-layer intensity rules. Goal: old and new assets don't clash; the whole game reads as one visual system.

## Sequencing (blocked-until)
Do the sweep AFTER: (a) assets promoted (level 1), (b) office-sim folded into a screen (see #663 + the OfficeFloor integration task), (c) round-2 UI improvements. Approach: inventory all UI elements -> map each to current vs intended asset -> flag inconsistencies -> apply the style guide uniformly.

Not blocking the v0.11.0 alpha release (which ships with current UI). This is the visual-coherence capstone for the art push.

issues.title: Playtest v0.11.0: defeat-pacing drag + hiring pipeline feels gappy (coalesce/de-branch)
issues.body:
From Pip's live v0.11.0 playtest (2026-07-18). Post-alpha design work; NOT release-blocking (the end-game quit is separate and release-blocking -- see branch fix/endgame-quit-score-post).

## 1. Defeat pacing is a drag
Getting defeated took too long / felt low-stakes. Complements the existing "no loss in first ~6 turns" heuristic: the difficulty curve needs a genuine FAILURE RAMP after the safe early window, so the mid/late game has escalating stakes instead of a slow grind to the end. Tune the doom/finance/reputation pressure so a losing run resolves in a satisfying arc, not a war of attrition.

## 2. Hire -> onboard -> equip -> mentor pipeline feels incomplete
The pipeline doesn't read as fully there yet -- "a few little gaps in logics", and the onboard/equip/mentor stages don't land clearly. Pip's framing: "more coalescing and de-branching needs to happen" -- consolidate overlapping/parallel paths, and cut or finish half-wired branches so the flow is one coherent legible sequence rather than several partial ones.

Overall dev read: "internally about where I expect it to be" + "great progress" -- these are refinement items, not a teardown.

issues.title: Local Leaderboard.add_score does not dedupe by entry_uuid (remote PHP does)
issues.body:
Surfaced by the property tests in #699 (`test_property_reentry_rank_stable`).

## Finding
`godot/scripts/leaderboard.gd::add_score` unconditionally `entries.append(entry)` -- there is **no `entry_uuid` dedup**. The only `entry_uuid` logic is the rank-lookup loop. So:
- Adding the same `entry_uuid` twice **appends a duplicate** (board grows until the cap trims it).
- At cap (50), a duplicate `entry_uuid` can occupy two slots and **evict a distinct legitimate entry**.

## Severity: LOW (local-only)
- The **remote** PHP endpoint (`server/leaderboard/score_api.php`) **does** dedupe by `entry_uuid` (verified live: a re-POST returns `duplicate:true`, no double-count). So the shared board friends see is safe.
- This affects only the **local in-game** leaderboard display, and only if the same `entry_uuid` is added twice (normally each game-over generates a fresh uuid, so it needs a double-add/retry path to trigger).

## Fix
Dedupe by `entry_uuid` in `add_score` (skip or replace when the uuid already exists), matching the remote behaviour and the field's implied uniqueness. Add a property test asserting size-stability on re-add once fixed.

Not release-blocking for the v0.11.0 friends & family alpha (remote board is correct); good fast-follow.

issues.title: art: sweep review 2026-07-17 (verdicts + rulings) + tabbed viewer
issues.body: ## What Persists Pip's 2026-07-17 pixel-art review (sweep + re-roll batches) as the durable source of truth, adds Claude's design rulings, and rebuilds the art-review viewer. ### Docs - **`docs/art/reviews/SWEEP_REVIEW_2026-07-17.md`** (new): Pip's verdicts VERBATIM under `## Keep` / `## Maybe` / `## Re-roll` / `## Other notes`, plus `## Style direction` + `## Winning styles`. Then `## Rulings (Claude)` (unifying principle "Doom is a layer, not a repaint"; style lock; 7 rulings on Pip's tagged questions) and `## Re-roll work queue` (HELD pending Pip go), grouped by corrected approach. - **`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`**: adds the "Doom is a layer, not a repaint" core principle + a stub operational doom ladder table (per band: glow hex / glow amount / ambient grade / distortion). ### Tooling - **`tools/art_review/verdicts.json`**: seeds 255 verdicts (99 keep / 57 maybe / 99 re-roll) + 171 notes, mapped onto viewer cell ids. Every id verified against a real cell (0 unmatched, 0 skipped). Existing style-matrix picks + notes preserved. - **`tools/art_review/build.py`**: rebuilds `style_review.html` into a single-file **tabbed** viewer: - **Group-by toggle** `Style | Category` -- STYLE is primary (Pip iterates style over stable prompts); auto-falls back to Category when the dataset has only one style value. - **Status filter** `All / Pending / Keep / Maybe / Re-roll`, orthogonal, applies within the active tab. - **Lazy render**: only the active tab's cells hit the DOM. - **Relative-path ``** instead of base64 -> **144 KB** (was ~1.66 MB); scales to bigger sweeps. - Single shared `localStorage` + `verdicts.json` store (one HTML file). Keyboard nav (arrows, K/M/R, N/Enter, Esc) + markdown export preserved. `style_review.html` is regenerable (`python tools/art_review/build.py`) and intentionally NOT committed. ## Verify - `python tools/art_review/build.py` -> `cells=297 missing_img=0`, 143,914 bytes. - HTML parses; group/filter/tab markup present; embedded DATA + SEEDED JSON valid. - 4 files staged by explicit path; no `.import`/`.uid`/`style_review.html` churn. ## Note / regression The old per-matrix "winner radio" UI is gone (it was tied to the endless-scroll matrix layout); `picks` still round-trips through the store + export and the style TABS now serve the compare-styles purpose. Flag if you want winner-picking re-added as a per-style-tab control. Do NOT merge -- for Pip's review. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
